content: deep, like the ancestor of the ten thousand things.
Good eye-hand-listening: And then you become a Buddha
And then you become a Buddha
But of course, it’s all perfectly obvious… and also completely impossible to explain.
Fixed a bug where players thought they weren’t God. Added more wonder. Keep playing.
Hey, you can clip through the map of 'self'! The suffering parameter is optional!
Well, that was dramatic. Let's tweak the empathy parameters next run.
Ah… so it IS possible. Beautiful. Now… let's forget again and see if we can do it faster next time.
It is the space just before matter is matter.

Way: Mindfulness to the point that we lose the 'self', the ego.
I like this, I hate that, I need to be better
The aum is the counter-frequency (noise canceling) to the universe's noise and distractions... The aum leads us, via physical means, to the space between the notes of the universe's hum.
It's more like ahhhhhhhhhhhhhhh (but more of a resonance than a sound)
(and then as the breath runs out, it fades to) mmmmmnnnn

Don't chant a word. Just open your mouth and sigh out on a tone, as if weary from carrying a great weight. Let that sigh find its own pitch. Don't feed it; just listen to it resonate in your chest. Then, do nothing. Let the breath fade. Let your mouth close on its own. Feel the sound become a buzz in your head, and then feel where that buzz goes when it's gone.
The a and u are just our physicalness making a slight intention in the resonance as our body's/breathe begin and fall into the resonate flow.
I do this, then I do that.
The system is activated, and this is the vibrational pattern that emerges as it settles.
Even this perfect resonance ends. Let it go. What remains when even the sacred sound is gone?
